key_insight: The paper shows that a steady 2D induction heating model is well-posed and that finite element convergence can be guaranteed, with a bound-preserving heat solver relaxing mesh and domain restrictions.
engineering_relevance: This provides a more reliable simulation pathway for induction heating applications where source terms are only weakly integrable and standard FEM may be fragile.
method: Justify the natural weak formulation using a priori regularity for the coupled PDEs.
Prove convergence of standard Galerkin FEM on convex domains with suitable mesh conditions.
Apply a bound-preserving method to the heat equation to obtain convergence under weaker geometric and mesh assumptions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical convergence proofs supported by numerical experiments that confirm the analysis and highlight the BPM advantage.
watch_item: The improved convergence guarantees still depend on problem- and mesh-specific conditions, so careful discretization and domain handling remain important.
paper_title: Analysis and approximation of a two-dimensional induction heating problem
